title: Multi-Agent Delegation, Context Minimization, Merge, and Failure Containment · Language Clinic: Delegation Fit
summary: A coordinator sends full user context and credentials to many agents, gives them shared write tools, accepts unvalidated summaries, and amplifies one poisoned result across the system. Predict authority, model and API behavior, state, tool effects, budgets, privacy, safety, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delegation fit increment in typed contained delegation graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
Verify tool confirmation, policy, evaluation, event, error, cancellation, and deployment behavior against framework-independent conformance cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-ai-agent-python cumulative safe evaluated AI agent portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python interaction, tool, policy, state, memory,
evaluation, telemetry, and recovery models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only.
Provider APIs, Gemini models, native google-genai, google-adk, Pydantic, HTTP, credentials,
host files, code execution, browsers, databases, telemetry backends, load, cost, privacy review,
and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentPolicy:
    stakeholder_task_id: str
    allowed_tool_names: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_steps: int = 12
    maximum_tool_calls: int = 8
    approval_required_for_writes: bool = True


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class AgentEvidence:
    revision: str
    model_config_digest: str
    policy_digest: str
    evaluation_digest: str
    recovery_drill_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Safe evaluated Python AI agent portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: agentpy-delegation-fit — Use delegation only when specialized context, independent work, isolation, or ownership beats a simpler function, workflow, tool, or single agent.
# Evidence: agentpy-worked-agentpy-multi-agent-delegation-3
# Competency: agentpy-worked-agentpy-multi-agent-delegation-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader interaction review.
# Operating constraint: every external effect requires explicit least-authority policy.
# Changed case: change one tool call, argument, authority, result, or failure and preserve correlation.
def worked_agentpy_multi_agent_delegation_3(delegated_tasks: int = 18, accepted_results: int = 12, rejected_results: int = 6, minimized_contexts: int = 18, cascading_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorityworked3",
        "interaction5798",
        "tool3dekrow",
        "stateworked35798",
        "evaluation5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if delegated_tasks < 6 or accepted_results < 0 or rejected_results < 0:
        return False, delegated_tasks, "delegation-count"
    if accepted_results + rejected_results != delegated_tasks:
        return False, accepted_results, "delegation-reconciliation"
    if minimized_contexts != delegated_tasks:
        return False, minimized_contexts, "delegation-context"
    if cascading_failures > 0:
        return False, cascading_failures, "delegation-cascade"
    assert accepted_results + rejected_results == delegated_tasks and cascading_failures == 0
    return True, accepted_results, "contained-agent-delegation"

content.code:
# Evidence: agentpy-delegation-fit-vywag2
# Competency: agentpy-delegation-fit.
# Case 3865: a multilingual case-routing evaluation.
# Operating constraint: last valid checkpoint, audit trail, kill switch, and rollback path must survive failure.
# Changed case: replace trusted context with untrusted indirect instructions and preserve data-versus-instruction boundaries.
def evidence_agentpy_delegation_fit_vywag2(delegated_tasks: int = 18, accepted_results: int = 12, rejected_results: int = 6, minimized_contexts: int = 18, cascading_failures: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vywag2 = "vywag2-2gawyv-3865"
    assert evidence_variant_vywag2
    evidence_axes_vywag2 = (
        "authorityvywag2",
        "interaction3865",
        "tool2gawyv",
        "statevywag23865",
        "evaluation3865vywag2",
        "recovery2gawyv3865",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vywag2)) == 6
    if delegated_tasks < 6 or accepted_results < 0 or rejected_results < 0:
        return False, delegated_tasks, "delegation-count"
    if accepted_results + rejected_results != delegated_tasks:
        return False, accepted_results, "delegation-reconciliation"
    if minimized_contexts != delegated_tasks:
        return False, minimized_contexts, "delegation-context"
    if cascading_failures > 0:
        return False, cascading_failures, "delegation-cascade"
    assert accepted_results + rejected_results == delegated_tasks and cascading_failures == 0
    return True, accepted_results, "contained-agent-delegation"
